params.name: Owl Stele
params.description: A Bronze Age artifact from the Iberian Peninsula, dating back approximately five thousand years. Traditionally, these stone plaques were thought to symbolize deities or the deceased. However, recent research suggests that these plaques may have been toys carved by children. Researchers analyzed a hundred stone plaques and compared them to modern children's drawings of owls, finding similarities between the two. The top of the plaques has two small holes, possibly used to insert feathers to mimic …
